name: Jenkins MCP Server
what_it_does: Jenkins MCP server enabling AI agents to interact with Jenkins CI/CD — querying build status and history, triggering pipeline jobs, reading console output, managing plugins, and integrating Jenkins automation into agent-driven DevOps workflows for build monitoring and pipeline orchestration.
use_cases: Triggering Jenkins builds from deployment agents
Checking build status and recent failures from monitoring agents
Reading pipeline console output for failure analysis from debugging agents
Querying job configurations from DevOps agents
Automated build management from CI/CD orchestration agents
Jenkins pipeline troubleshooting from NOC agents
not_for: Teams using GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, or CircleCI (different MCPs needed)
Highly security-sensitive Jenkins instances where external API access is restricted
best_when: A DevOps team runs Jenkins CI/CD and wants AI agents to monitor builds, trigger pipelines, and diagnose failures — integrating Jenkins into conversational DevOps workflows.
avoid_when: You use GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, or another CI/CD platform.

auth.notes: Jenkins API token (preferred) or username/password. Generate API token from Jenkins user profile. Jenkins URL must be configured.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Jenkins is completely free and open source
pricing.notes: Jenkins is free open source CI/CD. Community MCP is free. Infrastructure costs for Jenkins server apply.

performance.rate_limits: Local Jenkins API — no external rate limits

known_agent_gotchas: Jenkins API token is user-scoped — create dedicated service account
Build triggering requires appropriate Jenkins permissions
Console output for long builds can be very large
Community MCP — Jenkins API is well-documented but MCP coverage may be partial
Jenkins CSRF protection may require crumb tokens
Jenkins plugin ecosystem varies — not all features available on all instances
agent_readiness.security_notes: Jenkins API token. HTTPS recommended. Create service account with minimum permissions. Community MCP. Build triggering is privileged.

security_score_components.security_notes: Jenkins API token. HTTPS. Service account. Community MCP. Build triggers are privileged.
